value: Coherus Oncology is a commercial-stage U.S. oncology company whose only marketed product, LOQTORZI (toripalimab-tpzi), is the sole FDA-approved and NCCN-preferred immunotherapy for recurrent/metastatic nasopharyngeal carcinoma. BBDC owns a small $3.99M first-lien slice (plus $1.5M royalty rights) of Coherus's $38.7M senior-secured, interest-only 2029 Term Loan (SOFR+8%, 11.7% cash), a differentiated, well-collateralized credit marked above cost. Repayment is supported near-term by a net-cash balance sheet ($167.0M cash & marketable securities vs $38.8M funded debt), a $15M minimum-liquidity covenant with substantial headroom, first-lien collateral over substantially all assets including IP, and growing LOQTORZI revenue (FY2025 $40.8M, +113% YoY). Repayment could be impaired by sustained operating losses and cash burn (FY2025 operating loss $(181)M; heavy quarterly burn) that require recurring equity raises, extreme single-product/single-indication concentration, a newly FDA-approved competitor (penpulimab), and single-source China supply. The portfolio manager should monitor quarterly LOQTORZI revenue, normalized cash burn (ex-TSA) and unrestricted cash relative to the $15M covenant, the pace of equity issuance/ATM usage, and any U.S. launch by penpulimab as the earliest indicators of solvency or refinancing stress.
notes: Answers: what Coherus does; why BBDC owns it; what supports repayment; what could impair it; what to monitor now.
